verdict_reasoning: MAGEA4 is an intracellular cancer-testis antigen localized to the nucleus and cytoplasm. It lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any mechanism to reach the outer face of the plasma membrane. While MAGEA4-derived peptides are well-established MHC-I presentation targets recognized by CD8+ T cells (driving multiple TCR-T and TCR-mimic clinical programs), the protein body itself is not surface-accessible. MHC-peptide presentation is not credited as evidence for surface accessibility of the protein body.
